prerequisites: A Brex account with API access.
Sign in to dashboard.brex.com as an account admin or card admin, which is required to create a token.
Your company must accept the Brex developer API agreement before tokens can be generated.
A start_date value to define the earliest point from which data is synced.

authMethods.name: User token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Brex user token supplied in the user_token field. The token (prefixed bxt_) is passed as a Bearer credential in the Authorization header: 'Authorization: Bearer {user_token}'. Generate it from the Brex dashboard under Developer > Settings. Tokens expire if they are not used to make an API call for 90 days.

setupSteps.title: Create a Brex user token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to dashboard.brex.com as an account admin or card admin. Go to Developer > Settings, accept the developer API agreement if prompted, create a token, name it, and select the scopes (level of data access) your sync needs. Copy the generated token value, which begins with bxt_.

setupSteps.title: Provide the user token
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied token in the user_token field. This is the secret credential used to authenticate; it is sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header on every request to the Brex API.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which the connector should retrieve data.
